RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 300, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Sherri Gallick serves the people of District 62, which includes portions of Cass and Bates counties. First elected in 2022, she began serving her initial two-year term in 2023. She currently serves as Chair of the Economic Development Committee and Vice Chair of the Higher Education and Workforce Development Committee.
A proud graduate of the University of Missouri, Gallick holds a Bachelor of Science in Education and an MBA. She remains actively involved with her alma mater, having served on executive boards for 15+ years and as President of the Mizzou Alumni Association in 2015. She currently serves on the MAA Past Presidents board. Gallick is also a dedicated community leader, serving on the CASCO Board, which supports individuals with developmental disabilities. She volunteers with Uplift Kitchen and has served as a Court Appointed Special Advocate (CASA) for children in need.
Before joining the legislature, Gallick was an executive in the food industry, holding leadership positions with Jimmy Dean Foods and Maple Leaf Farms. Her business background continues to guide her work in the House. In recognition of her contributions, she was named Freshman of the Year in 2023 by the Missouri Chamber of Commerce and received a Courage Award from the Missouri Cattlemen’s Association. In 2025, she was honored with the Capitol Impact Award from the Missouri Grocers Association and the Defender of Business Award from the Missouri Chamber for her leadership in economic development and pro-business legislation.
Gallick and her husband, Randy, reside in Cass County. They have two married sons, one is with the Indiana Army National Guard, and the other is practicing law in Florida, Texas, and Washington, DC.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Authorizes a transient guest tax in Harrisonville upon voter approval
Requires schools to establish cardiac emergency response plans
Modifies provisions relating to the posting of notices
Establishes provisions relating to civil liability for publishing or distributing material harmful to minors on the internet
Modifies provisions governing the use of county developmental disability resource board tax levies
Modifies provisions governing the preemption of local ordinances involving employment law
Modifies provisions relating to licensed interior designers
Modifies provisions relating to employee compensation
Creates provisions relating to an exemption from liability for persons rendering emergency care at the scene of an accident or other emergency
Establishes the "Civil Liability for Employers Hiring Ex-Offenders Act", which provides liability protections for employers hiring those convicted of certain offenses
Authorizes a temporary extension of hours for alcoholic beverage sales by certain liquor licensees for the duration of the 2026 FIFA World Cup Tournament
Establishes the "Missouri Entertainment Facility - Capital Assistance Program"
Specifies that the department of health and senior services must not deny trauma center status to a hospital based on its distance from another trauma center

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Safe Place for Newborns Fund" for the installation of newborn safety incubators
Establishes provisions relating to electronic estate planning
Modifies provisions relating to human trafficking
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Modifies provisions relating to master's social work degree programs

Authorizes the "Child Care Contribution Tax Credit Act", the "Employer-Provided Child Care Assistance Tax Credit Act", and the "Child Care Providers Tax Credit", relating to tax credits for child care
Modifies provisions relating to motor vehicle assessment valuations
Authorizes a tax credit for certain charitable donations to local hospital foundations
Creates provisions relating to health care provider participation in health insurance plans
Prohibits local governments from requiring private property owners to accept Section 8 vouchers
Provides protections against discrimination and antisemitism in public schools and public postsecondary educational institutions
Changes provisions governing the compensation of circuit clerks
Authorizes the "Volunteer First Responder Tax Credit", relating to tax credits for certain first responders
Provides protections against discrimination and antisemitism in public schools and public postsecondary educational institutions
Modifies inspection and gauging fees imposed on barrels of malt liquor
Adds endangering the welfare of a child in the first degree to the definition of dangerous felony and modifies the offense of endangering the welfare of a child in the first degree
Modifies provisions relating to the age of marriage
Modifies the show MO act tax credit caps for qualified motion media production projects
Proposes a constitutional amendment to allow counties and cities to increase local sales taxes upon approval by the voters
